name: Pin&Play
short_description: A ubicomp networking technique that turns everyday surfaces into a two-dimensional power-and-data bus, so objects pinned to a wall or board with pushpin connectors gain network connectivity and power.
long_description: Pin&Play augments familiar surfaces such as walls and notice-boards with layered conductive sheets, turning the surface itself into a shared communication and power medium; objects are connected simply by pinning them with a pushpin-style connector. The authors demonstrated the idea with a smart notice-board, in which pinned notes, controls, and pictures become networked objects without wiring or configuration. It matters as physical AI / interaction design because it reframes attachment-by-pinning - an established everyday gesture - as the act of joining a network, making device ecologies ad hoc, spatial, and tangible rather than hidden in cabling.
ai_note: Pin&Play is a conductive-surface power and networking technique using pushpin connectors; no AI or ML runs in the system.

images.caption: Pin&Play smart notice-board: objects attached to a conductive surface via pushpin connectors receive power and network connectivity (research figure).
images.alt_text: Diagram of a notice-board surface with a pushpin connector attaching an object, illustrating how conductive layers carry power and data through the pin.

sources.title: Pin & Play: Networking Objects through Pins
